item.title: ‘Married at First Sight’ allegations shock UK TV industry
item.description: Two women have said they were raped during the filming of the Channel 4 dating show

item.title: Google debuts AI model as it comes under pressure from coding rivals
item.description: CEO Sundar Pichai says new Gemini model closes gap with Anthropic and OpenAI

item.title: Ebola raises questions over pandemic preparedness
item.description: Aid cuts and US hostility to concept of global public health endanger us all

item.title: Chanel to open more stores in China as new designer stirs ‘Blazy mania’
item.description: French luxury group reports a return to sales and profit growth

item.title: Putin and Xi to discuss huge gas project at China summit
item.description: Talks on Power of Siberia 2 pipeline come just days after Donald Trump’s visit to Beijing

item.title: Treasury pushes supermarkets to cap food prices
item.description: Retailers furious at government effort aimed at limiting impact of inflation

item.title: Wendy’s is a prime candidate for the Nelson Peltz activist special
item.description: One-third drop in the fast-food chain’s share price has created another opportunity for Trian

item.title: EU explores fertiliser stockpiling as food crisis looms
item.description: Supplies of crop nutrients have been disrupted by Middle East war

item.title: Deloitte announces bigger bonuses and more promotions after beating targets
item.description: Bonus pool rises by 14% and more than a quarter of UK staff will be elevated to new roles

item.title: HS2 to cost up to £102.7bn, government admits
item.description: MPs told that price tag for high-speed rail line has jumped by £20bn and that project will not be completed until 2040s

item.title: Deutsche Bank fined by UK for breaching Russia sanctions
item.description: OFSI fines lender £165,000 over payments linked to streamer Okko

item.title: Monzo boss pledges European expansion as profits jump
item.description: Diana Layfield pointed to ‘Monzo-shaped hole’ in markets across Europe

item.title: Solar’s day in the sun is rapidly approaching
item.description: Plus, why Citadel is rapidly expanding its LNG operations

item.title: Sweden selects French navy frigates in €3.7bn deal in blow to UK’s Babcock
item.description: Two countries deepen defence co-operation as Europe boosts military spending to deter Russia

item.title: Mango founder’s son detained over father’s mountain death
item.description: Billionaire Isak Andic died while hiking with son Jonathan in 2024

item.title: German government launches Uniper privatisation four years after rescue
item.description: Berlin to reduce stake in gas importer from 99% to 25% following nationalisation triggered by Ukraine war

item.title: Energy price cap to rise less than initially forecast
item.description: Analysts at Cornwall Insight predict a 13% increase for July to September

item.title: Crest Nicholson delays results as housebuilder seeks leniency from lenders
item.description: Shares in group have plummeted since profit warning last month

item.title: Standard Chartered to replace ‘lower-value human capital’ with AI
item.description: Comments from CEO Bill Winters come as bank sets out plans to cut almost 8,000 jobs

item.title: FirstFT: Big investors warn of market ‘correction’ risk
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Xi’s Putin comments and Israel’s land seizure

item.title: Lethal Ebola virus outbreak triggers urgent international quest for vaccine
item.description: World Health Organization experts will meet to recommend candidate jabs for clinical trials

item.title: A $420bn mega-merger for AI’s next-era dominion
item.description: ‘Data centre alley’ is at the centre of NextEra’s deal with Dominion

item.title: Drone start-up Helsing set to mount joint bid for military satellite project
item.description: German group to join up with OHB to build satellite constellation with AI to provide surveillance and reconnaissance

item.title: Worries on gilts point to wider bond market vulnerabilities
item.description: Rising public debt adds pressure while increased hedge fund trading opens up new fragilities

item.title: Astorg’s flagship fundraise struggles as private equity braces for shakeout
item.description: Investors reluctant to commit to new fund of French firm that has yet to sell prize asset

item.title: Inside the luxury towers behind Manchester’s revival
item.description: Salboy, the developer co-founded by the Betfred billionaire Fred Done, has helped to transform the city

item.title: Google DeepMind’s Demis Hassabis emerges as early Anthropic investor
item.description: Nobel laureate’s protégés are raising billions and spreading his influence across the AI industry

item.title: SpaceX IPO adds air to the Silicon Valley ‘genius bubble’
item.description: The biggest one-man brands today benefit from the impression that no cycle can bring them down

item.title: Iran stockpiles oil on ageing tankers anchored in the Gulf
item.description: Tehran turns to floating storage to keep country’s production going

item.title: Belfast Harbour unveils £1.3bn growth plan in bid to rival Dublin
item.description: Irish Sea port bets on freight, cruise and offshore wind businesses
